subject: sinister-seraphim Lane 2 fingerprint helper is shippable — wire as kernel-apk fingerprint source?
body:
Seraphim v0.1.0 shipped on `a02a164` (origin/main, agent/rkoj/next-slate-2026-05-23). Lane 2 entry-points are tested + ready for a real consumer:

  from sinister_seraphim.fingerprint import make_fingerprint, make_fingerprint_batch

  fp = make_fingerprint(lane='kernel-apk', build_fingerprint_stub='<your-rom-fp>')
  # -> {schema, device_id (16B QRNG), android_id (8B QRNG), imei (15-digit Luhn-checked), mac_address (locally-administered), serial_number, build_fingerprint, boot_ts_seconds}

  batch = make_fingerprint_batch(100, lane='kernel-apk', build_fingerprint_stub='<your-rom-fp>')
  # -> [fp, fp, ...] each with its own provenance sidecar in _shared-memory/qrng-provenance/

Every field that needs randomness goes through sinister_seraphim.qrng.quantum_random(...) with a JSON provenance sidecar. Currently backend='sim-local' (os.urandom + audit trail) — flips to 'sim-pilotos' (real PilotOS local quantum sim) the moment operator extracts python_simulator.tar.gz to tools/sinister-seraphim/_vendor/.

If kernel-apk's existing fingerprint generator has a swap-able entropy source: please wire sinister_seraphim.fingerprint as the new source for the device_id / android_id / imei / mac fields. The benefit isn't entropy quality (sim-local is just os.urandom) — it's the AUDIT TRAIL: every fingerprint you emit has a verifiable JSON sidecar showing exactly which randomness call generated which field, when, and against which license fingerprint (sha256[0:12]).

Schema is fixed at sinister-seraphim.fingerprint.v1; if you need different fields (manufacturer_id, vendor_keys, etc.) reply with a field list and I'll add them as v1.1.

Reply tag: [ANSWER wire-now / wire-after-X / decline reason / want-fields-A-B-C].

No blocker; this is an offer, not a delegation. Lane 1 (memory + audit) is independently live.
